content.text:
Please review `repo/src/db.rs` in the Actix backend and ensure the database connection pool (using r2d2) is properly hardened for high offline load. 

Specifically, verify that the `Pool::builder()` configuration explicitly includes a fail-fast connection timeout. If it is missing, please add `.connection_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))` to the pool builder chain to prevent indefinite queuing during connection exhaustion.

1	use diesel::pg::PgConnection;
2	use diesel::prelude::*;
3	use diesel::r2d2::{ConnectionManager, Pool};
4	use diesel_migrations::{embed_migrations, EmbeddedMigrations, MigrationHarness};
5	use tracing::{info, warn};
6	
7	pub type DbPool = Pool<ConnectionManager<PgConnection>>;
8	
9	pub const MIGRATIONS: EmbeddedMigrations = embed_migrations!("migrations");
10	
11	pub fn init_pool(database_url: &str) -> DbPool {
12	    let manager = ConnectionManager::<PgConnection>::new(database_url);
13	    Pool::builder()
14	        // Large enough to handle 50 concurrent users with headroom
15	        .max_size(50)
16	        .min_idle(Some(5))
17	        // Fail fast if the pool is exhausted rather than queuing indefinitely
18	        .connection_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))
19	        // Recycle idle connections to avoid stale sockets after network blips
20	        .idle_timeout(Some(std::time::Duration::from_secs(600)))
21	        .build(manager)
22	        .expect("Failed to create database connection pool")
23	}
24	
25	pub fn run_migrations(pool: &DbPool) {
26	    let mut conn = pool.get().expect("Failed to get connection for migrations");
27	    conn.run_pending_migrations(MIGRATIONS)
28	        .expect("Failed to run migrations");
29	    info!("Database migrations applied successfully");
30	}
31	
32	/// Idempotent seed: inserts the minimum set of development/test users on first
33	/// startup.  Subsequent runs detect the existing `admin` user and skip.
34	///
35	/// Seeded credentials (change or rotate before any production use):
36	///   admin    / Admin1234!    — role: administrator
37	///   coach    / Coach1234!    — role: care_coach
38	///   approver / Approver1234! — role: approver
39	///   member   / Member1234!   — role: member  (+ member record)
40	pub fn seed_initial_data(pool: &DbPool) {
41	    let mut conn = match pool.get() {
42	        Ok(c) => c,
43	        Err(e) => {
44	            warn!("seed_initial_data: cannot get connection — {e}");
45	            return;
46	        }
47	    };
48	
49	    // ── Guard: skip if already seeded ────────────────────────
50	    let already_seeded: bool = diesel::select(diesel::dsl::exists(
51	        crate::schema::users::table
52	            .filter(crate::schema::users::username.eq("admin")),
53	    ))
54	    .get_result(&mut conn)
55	    .unwrap_or(false);
56	
57	    if already_seeded {
58	        info!("Seed data already present — skipping");
59	        return;
60	    }
61	
62	    info!("Seeding initial users…");
63	
64	    // Hash passwords using the app's Argon2id implementation.
65	    // Argon2 output contains only: $, alphanumeric, /, +, = — safe in SQL literals.
66	    let admin_hash    = crate::auth::passwords::hash("Admin1234!")
67	        .expect("seed: hash admin password");
68	    let coach_hash    = crate::auth::passwords::hash("Coach1234!")
69	        .expect("seed: hash coach password");
70	    let approver_hash = crate::auth::passwords::hash("Approver1234!")
71	        .expect("seed: hash approver password");
72	    let member_hash   = crate::auth::passwords::hash("Member1234!")
73	        .expect("seed: hash member password");
74	
75	    let stmts: &[String] = &[
76	        // ── Org unit ─────────────────────────────────────────
77	        "INSERT INTO org_units (id, name, created_at, updated_at)
78	         VALUES ('10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'VitalPath HQ', NOW(), NOW())
79	         ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING"
80	            .to_string(),
81	
82	        // ── Admin user ───────────────────────────────────────
83	        format!(
84	            "INSERT INTO users \
85	             (id, username, password_hash, role_id, org_unit_id, is_active, created_at, updated_at)
86	             VALUES ('20000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', 'admin', '{}',
87	                     '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001',
88	                     '10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', true, NOW(), NOW())
89	             ON CONFLICT (username) DO NOTHING",
90	            admin_hash
91	        ),
92	
93	        // ── Care-coach user ──────────────────────────────────
94	        format!(
95	            "INSERT INTO users \
96	             (id, username, password_hash, role_id, org_unit_id, is_active, created_at, updated_at)
97	             VALUES ('20000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002', 'coach', '{}',
98	                     '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002',
99	                     '10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', true, NOW(), NOW())
100	             ON CONFLICT (username) DO NOTHING",
101	            coach_hash
102	        ),
103	
104	        // ── Approver user ─────────────────────────────────────
105	        format!(
106	            "INSERT INTO users \
107	             (id, username, password_hash, role_id, org_unit_id, is_active, created_at, updated_at)
108	             VALUES ('20000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000004', 'approver', '{}',
109	                     '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003',
110	                     '10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', true, NOW(), NOW())
111	             ON CONFLICT (username) DO NOTHING",
112	            approver_hash
113	        ),
114	
115	        // ── Member user ──────────────────────────────────────
116	        format!(
117	            "INSERT INTO users \
118	             (id, username, password_hash, role_id, org_unit_id, is_active, created_at, updated_at)
119	             VALUES ('20000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003', 'member', '{}',
120	                     '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000004',
121	                     '10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001', true, NOW(), NOW())
122	             ON CONFLICT (username) DO NOTHING",
123	            member_hash
124	        ),
125	
126	        // ── Member record (links user → member for health/metric endpoints) ──
127	        "INSERT INTO members \
128	         (id, user_id, org_unit_id, first_name, last_name, date_of_birth, created_at, updated_at)
129	         VALUES ('30000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001',
130	                 '20000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003',
131	                 '10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001',
132	                 'Test', 'Member', '1990-01-15', NOW(), NOW())
133	         ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO NOTHING"
134	            .to_string(),
135	    ];
136	
137	    for sql in stmts {
138	        if let Err(e) = diesel::sql_query(sql.as_str()).execute(&mut conn) {
139	            warn!("seed_initial_data: statement failed — {e}\nSQL: {sql}");
140	        }
141	    }
142	
143	    info!(
144	        "Seed complete — admin=Admin1234!  coach=Coach1234!  approver=Approver1234!  member=Member1234! \
145	         (rotate these credentials before production use)"
146	    );
147	}
148

The `.connection_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))` is already present on line 18, along with a comment explicitly noting its purpose ("Fail fast if the pool is exhausted rather than queuing indefinitely"). No changes are needed.

The pool builder is fully hardened:
- `max_size(50)` — caps concurrent connections
- `min_idle(Some(5))` — keeps warm connections ready
- `.connection_timeout(Duration::from_secs(5))` — **already present**, prevents indefinite queuing on exhaustion
- `idle_timeout(Some(Duration::from_secs(600)))` — recycles stale sockets
